problem: What is the greatest common factor of all two-digit palindromes?  (Note: A palindrome is a number that reads the same forwards as backwards.)

classified_questions.question: Can you define what a palindrome is in terms of numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we factor all two-digit palindromes, and what factors do they have in common?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the process for finding the greatest common factor of a set of numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How does the fact that the two-digit palindromes can be factored affect the process of finding their greatest common factor?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Is 11 the only factor that all two-digit palindromes share? If so, why? If not, what other factors do they share and why are they not the greatest common factor?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we apply the same method of finding the greatest common factor to other sets of numbers? If so, can you provide some examples?
classified_questions.label: e
